compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Pass-4 coaching (move registry + deterministically rendered notes + guide_url deep-links into docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md) currently runs only over SURVIVING ADVISORY findings. Blocking findings - the ones an agent MUST remediate before claim - get no coaching note and no guide_url. Field data: the E6/F1 oracle-strictness finding family recurred 3-5 consecutive rounds on tickets fde0/0ee8/76d2 because agents reworded ACs instead of adding the demanded executable oracle; a guide link plus a move-shaped nudge on the block itself is the cheapest one-round-remediation lever. The reviewer/writer separation must be preserved exactly as-is: moves come from the locked registry, prose is rendered deterministically, the C1 subject validator still rejects code/imperatives/solutions.

## What
Coaching over blocking findings must be widened at BOTH execution seams, plus the prompt, sidecar slim, and renderer. Grounded current shape: in src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml the `coach_inputs` step (op `plan_review_coach_inputs`, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:360) is bound `surviving: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}`; the `coach_gate` branch fires on `when: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}`; the `coach_notes` LLM step (prompt plan-review-coach) is bound `surviving: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.surfaced }}`; and the `coach` op (`plan_review_coach`, workflow_ops.py:475) already receives `blocking` but derives its move-trigger set from `ctx.inputs.get("surfaced")` only. The changes:
- `plan_review_coach_inputs` (workflow_ops.py:360): accept a new `blocking` input, compute `coachable = blocking + surviving` (blocking first), build instructions and triggers from `coachable`, and emit it as a new `findings` output alongside {plan, instructions}. The op's output schema src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_coach_inputs_output.schema.json is CLOSED (`additionalProperties: false`, properties {plan, instructions}) - add `"findings": {"type": "array", "description": "Blocking + surviving findings (blocking first) fed to coach_gate and the coach prompt."}` to its properties (keeping additionalProperties: false - the closure is deliberate).
- plan-review.yaml: the coach_inputs/coach_gate/coach_notes/coach block exists TWICE, structurally identical - once in the verify_branch then-arm (code-grounded/agentic path, ~lines 174-226) and once in the else-arm (non-agentic path, ~lines 252-304). Apply the SAME three edits to BOTH arms: add `blocking: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.blocking }}` to the `coach_inputs` step's `with:`; change `coach_gate`'s `when:` to `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}` (fires when EITHER bucket is non-empty); change `coach_notes`'s `surviving:` binding to `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}`. A unit test asserts the two arms' coach blocks remain binding-identical.
- `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475): derive the trigger set from the union of `blocking` + `surfaced` (it already receives both), matching coach_inputs.
- Decision tag: `render_coach_notes` (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:162) gains an optional decision map (finding_ref -> "block"|"advisory") stamped onto each rendered entry; the kernel `coach()` (coach.py:195, which invokes render_coach_notes internally at :217) gains a pass-through parameter for it. Call paths: `pass4_coach` (passes.py:842) reaches it via `review_kernel.coach(surviving, registry, pick=..., ...)` - it passes the map there; the workflow op `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475) calls `passes.render_coach_notes` directly and passes the map itself. Every coaching entry then carries `decision`. NOTE the live production path is EXCLUSIVELY the workflow ops above - `pass4_coach` in passes.py (:842) is exercised only by unit tests; it is widened identically anyway so the test path keeps production semantics, with a code comment stating that scope. Concretely: the kernel `coach()` (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:195) gains `blocking: Sequence = ()` and the decision-map pass-through as keyword parameters, computes `coachable = list(blocking) + list(surviving)`, widens its early-return gate from `if not surviving` (coach.py:211) to `if not coachable`, and passes `coachable` where it passed `surviving` (listing + render); `pass4_coach` gains a matching `blocking` keyword (default ()) it forwards to `review_kernel.coach()`.
- Sidecar slim: `build_payload` in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py slims coaching entries to a fixed field list {move_id, move_name, subject, finding_refs, coaching} (lines ~445-453) - add `decision` to that list so the tag survives persistence (additive optional field).
- Prompt + header: src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md reframed decision-neutral in BOTH places it anchors on advisory - the framing sentence AND the per-finding instruction at line 13 ("For each SURVIVING advisory finding..." becomes "For each finding in the list below..."), and the hardcoded listing header `## Surviving advisory findings (by id)` in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:123 becomes decision-neutral ("## Surviving findings (by id)").
- guide_url: the passthrough attach at src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:515-530 already keys off coaching entries' criteria - blocking entries get it for free once they exist; verify by test.
- CLI: NO renderer change - `_render_plan_review_text` in src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py already renders `coaching[]` as a flat block after the findings, so once the coach emits entries for blocking findings they render with their guide links through the existing loop. The CLI test below pins that behavior.

## Scope
IN: the widening above, decision tag, prompt/header rewording, sidecar slim addition, CLI rendering, unit tests. OUT: any change to move-registry contents, the subject validator, blocking/advisory decisions, thresholds, or the sidecar schema beyond the optional decision field. OUT: cross-finding conflict detection (parked epic grumpy-gland-rose).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A plan review whose verdict contains blocking findings emits a coaching entry (move_id + rendered note + guide_url) for each blocking finding. Oracle: two unit test cases - (a) 1 block + 1 advisory yields 2 coaching entries, where the block's entry carries decision="block" and guide_url and the advisory's carries decision="advisory" and guide_url; (b) 1 block + 0 advisories (the motivating BLOCK-only case, where today's coach_gate never fires) yields 1 coaching entry with decision="block" and guide_url; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "coach" -x` green.
- [ ] The two YAML arms' coach blocks remain binding-identical after the edits. Oracle: unit test in tests/unit/workflow/ parsing src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml and asserting the then-arm and else-arm coach_inputs/coach_gate/coach_notes/coach step bindings are equal; `python -m pytest tests/unit/workflow -k "coach" -x` green.
- [ ] The workflow path produces the same widening. Oracle: unit test (tests/unit/workflow/) stubbing a StepContext for `plan_review_coach_inputs` with 1 blocking + 1 surfaced finding, asserting the `findings` output contains both and the instructions listing names both; and a `plan_review_coach` test asserting a note referencing the blocking finding renders with decision="block".
- [ ] The coach prompt and listing header no longer frame the input set as advisory-only. Oracle: `grep -niP "advisory\s+finding" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md` returns no hits, AND a unit test asserts the rendered listing header for a mixed set is decision-neutral ("Surviving findings").
- [ ] Coaching entries for blocking findings survive sidecar persistence with their decision tag. Oracle: unit test over `build_payload` asserting a coaching entry with decision="block" retains that field in the emitted payload.
- [ ] Blocking-finding coaching notes pass the same C1 subject validation as advisory notes. Oracle: existing subject-validator tests extended to a blocking-finding case; suite green.
- [ ] `rebar review-plan` output for a BLOCK verdict includes the blocking finding's coaching line + guide link in the existing flat coaching block (no positional per-finding grouping is added). Oracle: CLI rendering unit test over `_render_plan_review_text` asserting the rendered text contains the blocking finding's coaching text and guide_url.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all pass.

file_impact.reason: widen bespoke pass-4 set to blocks

compiled_state.title: Extend Pass-4 coaching + guide_url to blocking findings
